---
title: "Imidacloprid BRM"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  word_document: default
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
date: "2025-08-09"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
# Load the libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(tidybayes)
library(ggplot2)
library(brms)
```
```{r}
imidacloprid.survival <- read.csv("Imidacloprid.survival2.csv")
str(imidacloprid.survival)
imidacloprid.survival <- imidacloprid.survival %>%
  mutate(DOSE = factor(DOSE, levels = c("control", "low", "high")))
```
```{r}
bayes_surv_model <- brm(
  formula = SURVIVAL | cens(1 - STATUS) ~ DOSE,
  family = weibull(),
  data = imidacloprid.survival,
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  iter = 4000,
  warmup = 1000,
  seed = 123,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95))
```

```{r}
summary(bayes_surv_model)
plot(bayes_surv_model)
```
```{r}
# Get posterior draws
posterior <- posterior_samples(bayes_surv_model)

# Or using tidybayes
bayes_surv_model %>%
  spread_draws(b_Intercept) %>%
  median_qi()
bayes_surv_model %>%
  spread_draws(b_DOSElow) %>%
  median_qi()

bayes_surv_model %>%
  spread_draws(b_DOSEhigh) %>%
  median_qi()
```
```{r}
posterior_survival <- posterior_predict(bayes_surv_model, 
                                       newdata = data.frame(DOSE = c("control", "low", "high")),
                                       re_formula = NULL)
```


```{r}
# Fit a brms model (for example)
fit <- brm(SURVIVAL ~ DOSE + (1|ID), data = imidacloprid.survival)
```


```{r}
ls()  # Lists all objects in your R environment
```

```{r}
# Extract posterior samples for each treatment group
posterior_samples <- as.data.frame(fit)

# Extract shape and scale parameters for each treatment group (adjust variable names if necessary)
control_shape_param <- posterior_samples$b_grouplow   # Replace 'b_grouplow' with actual variable for control
control_scale_param <- posterior_samples$sigma         # Replace 'sigma' with the scale parameter

# If you have 'low' and 'high' dose groups, extract them similarly:
low_shape_param <- posterior_samples$b_grouplow
low_scale_param <- posterior_samples$sigma

high_shape_param <- posterior_samples$b_grouphigh
high_scale_param <- posterior_samples$sigma
```


```{r}
names(as_draws_df(fit))
```

```{r}
fit_weib <- brm(
  SURVIVAL | cens(1 - STATUS) ~ DOSE, 
  data = imidacloprid.survival,
  family = weibull(),
  chains = 4, iter = 8000, warmup = 2000, cores = 4,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999, max_treedepth = 15),
  seed = 123)
```

```{r}
post <- as_draws_df(fit_weib)  # replace fit_weib with your model name
names(post)  # check column names to confirm
```
```{r}
# Shape is the same for all groups unless modeled as varying
shape_param <- post$shape

# Scale = exp(Intercept + effect)
scale_control <- exp(post$b_Intercept)
scale_low     <- exp(post$b_Intercept + post$b_DOSElow)
scale_high    <- exp(post$b_Intercept + post$b_DOSEhigh)
```

```{r}
scale_control
```


```{r}
time_vector <- seq(0, 21, by = 1)  # days in your experiment
num_draws <- length(shape_param)

# Empty matrices: rows = posterior draws, cols = time points
control_survival <- matrix(NA, nrow = num_draws, ncol = length(time_vector))
low_survival     <- control_survival
high_survival    <- control_survival

# Loop over posterior draws
for (i in 1:num_draws) {
  control_survival[i, ] <- exp(-(time_vector / scale_control[i]) ^ shape_param[i])
  low_survival[i, ]     <- exp(-(time_vector / scale_low[i])     ^ shape_param[i])
  high_survival[i, ]    <- exp(-(time_vector / scale_high[i])    ^ shape_param[i])
}
```

```{r}
# Mean survival curve
mean_control <- colMeans(control_survival)
mean_low     <- colMeans(low_survival)
mean_high    <- colMeans(high_survival)

# 95% credible intervals
ci_control <- apply(control_survival, 2, quantile, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
ci_low     <- apply(low_survival,     2, quantile, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
ci_high    <- apply(high_survival,    2, quantile, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
```

```{r}
mean_control
mean_low
mean_high
```
```{r}
ci_control
ci_low  
ci_high
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

plot_df <- data.frame(
  time = rep(time_vector, 3),
  mean_surv = c(mean_control, mean_low, mean_high),
  lower = c(ci_control[1,], ci_low[1,], ci_high[1,]),
  upper = c(ci_control[2,], ci_low[2,], ci_high[2,]),
  group = rep(c("Control", "Low", "High"), each = length(time_vector))
)

ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = time, y = mean_surv, color = group, fill = group)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  labs(x = "Days", y = "Survival Probability") +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
library(posterior)

# Convert model draws to a data frame
post <- as_draws_df(bayes_surv_model)

# Find empty or zero-length names
bad_cols <- which(names(post) == "" | is.na(names(post)))

if(length(bad_cols) > 0){
  names(post)[bad_cols] <- paste0("unnamed_col_", bad_cols)
}

# Now run your HR calculations with cleaned names:
b_low <- post$b_DOSElow
b_high <- post$b_DOSEhigh
shape <- post$shape

HR_low <- exp(-b_low * shape)
HR_high <- exp(-b_high * shape)
HR_high_vs_low <- HR_high / HR_low

summary_df <- tibble::tibble(
  comparison = c("Low vs Control", "High vs Control", "High vs Low"),
  median = c(median(HR_low), median(HR_high), median(HR_high_vs_low)),
  lower = c(quantile(HR_low, 0.025), quantile(HR_high, 0.025), quantile(HR_high_vs_low, 0.025)),
  upper = c(quantile(HR_low, 0.975), quantile(HR_high, 0.975), quantile(HR_high_vs_low, 0.975))
)
print(summary_df)
```

```{r}
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Your hazard ratio summary data
summary_df <- tibble(
  comparison = c("Low vs Control", "High vs Control", "High vs Low"),
  median = c(63.56, 6.62, 0.11),
  lower = c(3.65, 0.83, 0.0093),
  upper = c(3787.67, 193.29, 0.88)
)
```

```{r}
ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = comparison, y = median)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.15, color = "darkblue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_y_log10() +  # log scale to better visualize large range of HRs
  labs(
    title = "Hazard Ratios for Frog Survival by Dose Group",
    x = "Comparison",
    y = "Hazard Ratio (log scale)",
    caption = "Red dashed line = HR = 1 (no effect)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    plot.caption = element_text(face = "italic")
  )
```

```{r}
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{r}
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

summary_df %>%
  mutate(
    median = round(median, 2),
    lower = signif(lower, 3),
    upper = signif(upper, 3),
    `95% CI` = paste0("[", lower, ", ", upper, "]")
  ) %>%
  select(comparison, median, `95% CI`) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Summary of Hazard Ratios with 95% Credible Intervals",
    col.names = c("Comparison", "Median HR", "95% Credible Interval"),
    align = c("l", "r", "l")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    full_width = FALSE, 
    position = "center", 
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(2:3, bold = TRUE)
```

```{r}
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = time, y = mean_surv, color = group, fill = group)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.25, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Control" = "#d73027", 
                                 "Low" = "#4575b4", 
                                 "High" = "#1a9850")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#d73027", 
                                "Low" = "#4575b4", 
                                "High" = "#1a9850")) +
  labs(x = "Days", y = "Survival Probability", color = "Group", fill = "Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidybayes)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Extract posterior draws
post <- as_draws_df(bayes_surv_model)

# Shape and scale for each group
shape <- post$shape
scale_control <- exp(post$b_Intercept)
scale_low     <- exp(post$b_Intercept + post$b_DOSElow)
scale_high    <- exp(post$b_Intercept + post$b_DOSEhigh)

# Function: Restricted Mean Survival Time for Weibull
rmst_weibull <- function(tmax, scale, shape) {
  scale / shape * gamma_inc(1/shape, (tmax / scale)^shape)
}

# Incomplete gamma function wrapper
gamma_inc <- function(a, x) {
  gamma(a) * pgamma(x, shape = a, lower.tail = TRUE)
}

# Maximum follow-up time (21 days)
tmax <- 21

# Compute RMST for each posterior draw
rmst_control <- rmst_weibull(tmax, scale_control, shape)
rmst_low     <- rmst_weibull(tmax, scale_low, shape)
rmst_high    <- rmst_weibull(tmax, scale_high, shape)

# Combine into tidy data
rmst_df <- tibble(
  Group = rep(c("Control", "Low", "High"), each = length(rmst_control)),
  RMST = c(rmst_control, rmst_low, rmst_high)
)

# Summarize posterior
summary_table <- rmst_df %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  median_qi(RMST, .width = 0.95) %>%
  rename(
    Mean = RMST,
    Lower95 = .lower,
    Upper95 = .upper
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Mean = round(Mean, 2),
    Lower95 = round(Lower95, 2),
    Upper95 = round(Upper95, 2)
  ) %>%
  select(Group, Mean, Lower95, Upper95)

# Print styled kable table
summary_table %>%
  kable("html", caption = "Posterior Restricted Mean Survival Time (days) with 95% Credible Intervals",
        col.names = c("Group", "Mean", "Lower 95%", "Upper 95%")) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "center", bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(2:4, bold = TRUE)
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Posterior distribution plot for RMST
ggplot(rmst_df, aes(x = RMST, y = Group, fill = Group)) +
  stat_halfeye(.width = 0.95, point_interval = median_qi, 
               slab_alpha = 0.6, fatten_point = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "Posterior RMST (days) with 95% Credible Intervals",
    x = "Restricted Mean Survival Time (days)",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```
